semanticSearchField: Botanical name: Blackstonia perfoliata. Preferred Common Name: yellowwort. Also known as: canri felen. Family: Gentianaceae. Genus: Blackstonia. Description: An annual to 40cm tall, mainly found on chalky and limestone grasslands, sand dunes and in rocky places, with waxy, grey to bluish-green opposite pointed leaves fused around the stem.  Butter yellow, cup-shaped flowers with 6 to 8 petals, closing in the afternoon, are produced in summer and autumn. Cultivation: Grow in well-drained neutral to alkaline soil in full sun; a good coastal plant. Characteristics: plant type: Annual/Biennial, height: 0.1-0.5 metres, spread: 0.1-0.5 metres, foliage: Deciduous, sunlight requirements: Full sun, soil type: Chalk, Loam, Sand, aspect: South-facing, West-facing, hardiness: H7 (very hardy), moisture requirements: Well-drained, pH requirements: Alkaline, Neutral, habit: Clump-forming, time to full height: 1-2 years, exposure: Exposed, Sheltered. Colors and features: flowers: Yellow in Summer and  Autumn, foliage color: Pale Grey/Silver, Blue, Green in Spring, Summer and  Autumn. Suggested uses: Coastal, Cottage/Informal Garden, Wildflower meadow, Wildlife Gardens. Care information: pruning: No pruning required, propagation: Propagate by seed, pest resistance: Generally pest-free, disease resistance: Generally disease-free. Special features: native plant, perfect for pollinators
cultivation: Grow in well-drained neutral to alkaline soil in full sun; a good coastal plant
